commit.message:
Add full finance toolkit — 7 tools in one app

- app.py: home page with tool overview
- pages/1: 5-Year Financial Model (moved from root)
- pages/2: Annual Budget (existing)
- pages/3: Lending Calculator (loan + mortgage with early repayment)
- pages/4: Portfolio Analysis (Sharpe, VaR, risk/return scatter)
- pages/5: Stock Comparison (2 stocks, correlation, ratios)
- pages/6: Commodities Dashboard (gold, silver, oil, gas, copper, wheat)
- pages/7: Stock Analysis (any ticker, MAs, volatility, company info)

All charts rebuilt in Plotly. Consistent styling across all pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Annual Budget tool (1-year P&L with monthly breakdown)

- Sales forecast, direct costs, overheads by category
- Monthly P&L table with FY totals and % of revenue
- KPI cards: Revenue, Gross Profit, Overheads, EBITDA
- 4 charts: Revenue vs Costs, Monthly EBITDA, Overhead breakdown, Cumulative EBITDA
- Excel template download + upload
- PDF export with branded report

Dynamic rise distance: each row rises from proportional depth below base

Bottom row rises 40px, top row rises (N-1)*50+40px — words physically
travel from the foundation level up to their final position.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Increase towerRowIn translateY for more dramatic rise from base

60px travel distance makes each row visibly rise up from below
instead of barely-noticeable 20px nudge.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Iterate placement loop bottom→top so base rows (wide, appear first in
animation) win ties over observation deck rows. Big/prominent words now
land at the foundation and visibly appear first as the tower builds up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend profanity filter with anatomical and explicit terms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Lab11b: reset clears widget values, all buttons orange

Reset Answer: replaced pop(key) with a reset_flag pattern — set a flag
before st.rerun(), then on the next run assign the value to session_state
before the widget renders (Streamlit only allows widget state to be set
before the widget is instantiated, not after).

Button colors: removed the secondary-button grey CSS override so all
buttons inherit the orange baseline style.

Prevent iOS Safari back gesture during horizontal scroll

- Add overscroll-behavior-x: contain to horizontal scroll containers
- Add touch-action: pan-x pinch-zoom to capture horizontal swipes

when user is scrolling through treatment cases horizontally.

commit.message:
bak9: all screens locked — nav bars fixed, dispatch/oracle redesign complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: convert 4 GitHub Actions workflows from shell to TypeScript crux commands (#1692)

* fix(security): consolidate isSafeUrl and guard all URL rendering against XSS

- Add single canonical `isSafeUrl()` in `resource-utils.ts` (already existed)
- Remove 2 duplicate local implementations from `InlineCitationCards.tsx` and
  `FootnoteTooltip.tsx`; replace with imports from `resource-utils`
- Remove duplicate exported impl from `CitationOverlay.tsx`; replace with
  import + re-export from `resource-utils` (backwards compat for tests)
- Update `citation-data.test.ts` to import `isSafeUrl` from canonical source
- Guard all `<a href={url}>` in `References.tsx` — fallback title link and
  expanded external link section now require `isSafeUrl()` to pass
- Guard all `<a href={resource.url}>` in `ResourceLink.tsx` — both the main
  link and tooltip Source button; unsafe URLs render as plain text
- Guard `<a href={url}>` in `InfoBox.tsx` — Website field and external links
  (Wikipedia, LessWrong, etc.) now require `isSafeUrl()` to pass
- Guard `<a href={research.url}>` in `Crux.tsx` — relevant research links
  now require `isSafeUrl()` to pass

Closes #1661

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Create Release PR workflow and CI coverage for production PRs

Add a new GitHub Action (create-release-pr.yml) that creates standardized
release PRs from main → production with auto-generated changelogs grouped
by conventional commit type. The workflow is idempotent (updates existing
open release PRs) and handles same-day re-releases with incremented titles.

Also add `production` to CI's pull_request trigger branches so release PRs
get CI validation before merge.

Filed #1679 for investigating whether scheduled-deploy.yml is broken after
the production-branch switch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: harden release PR workflow shell safety

- Quote all ${{ }} interpolations for defense-in-depth
- Use $RUNNER_TEMP instead of /tmp for CI file isolation
- Pipe gh output through standalone jq with --arg for safe filtering
- Quote all ${RUNNER_TEMP} path references

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add shared crux/lib/git.ts helpers for workflow-to-TS conversions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: move release PR logic from shell to TypeScript crux command

Replace ~200 lines of fragile YAML shell scripting with a typed TypeScript
crux command (crux/commands/release.ts). The workflow YAML is now ~45 lines
that just calls `pnpm crux release create`. Adds 15 unit tests for commit
categorization and changelog generation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: convert 4 GitHub Actions workflows from shell to TypeScript

Move ~2,100 lines of YAML-embedded shell scripts into typed, tested
TypeScript crux CLI commands:

- auto-rebase.yml (152→50 lines) → `pnpm crux pr rebase-all`
- wellness-check.yml (1121→60 lines) → `pnpm crux health check --report --auto-issue`
- auto-update.yml (399→121 lines) → `pnpm crux auto-update run-ci`
- resolve-conflicts.yml (546→68 lines) → `pnpm crux pr resolve-conflicts`

New files:
- crux/lib/pr-rebase.ts — PR rebase orchestration with 4 safeguards
- crux/health/checks/job-queue.ts — job queue health check
- crux/health/checks/pr-quality.ts — PR quality + stuck label cleanup
- crux/health/wellness-report.ts — aggregate reporting + issue management
- crux/auto-update/ci-orchestrate.ts — full CI orchestration wrapper
- crux/lib/conflict-resolution.ts — 2-tier conflict resolution orchestration

All logic now has TypeScript type checking, vitest tests (104 new tests),
and can be run locally via `pnpm crux <command>`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use snake_case field names for GitHub REST API in pr-quality check

The GitHub REST API returns `created_at`/`updated_at` (snake_case), but
the interfaces used `createdAt`/`updatedAt` (camelCase). This caused all
time-based checks (stale PRs, stuck labels) to silently never trigger
because the fields would always be undefined.

Also fixed bug count query using per_page=1 which only reported 0 or 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: include spouse RRSP/TFSA in estate calculation and AI context

Estate engine was only using primary RRSP/TFSA, undercounting couple
estate by the spouse's balances. Dashboard and estate AI context sent
zero spouse data to Gemini, causing single-person insights for couples.

- estateEngine: add spouse RRSP/TFSA to grossEstate, breakdown, result
- buildAiData: add 9 spouse fields to dashboard, 2 to estate context
- EstateSummaryCards: show spouse assets in tooltip and bar chart
- 21 new tests covering all three fixes

Eliminate silent fallbacks, remove dead code, rename featurization modules

- Remove dead re-exports (_BOND_TYPE_MAP, _ENV_MAP, get_bond_type_matrix,
  one_hot_vector) from featurization/__init__.py
- Remove no-op prepare_data() override in ConformerEnsembleDataModule
- Remove OldCode/ reference from CLAUDE.md (directory no longer exists)
- Replace all silent fallbacks with explicit errors:
  - dataset.py: require rep_frame_idxs key in cache, raise on missing env
  - dataset.py: raise on unknown split assignment or missing molecule in split
  - mol_featurizer.py: raise if CSV missing rep frame column
  - pdb_loader.py: let OSError propagate, raise on empty MODEL blocks
  - cycloformer_model.py: require atom_mask and conformer_mask in batch
  - main_train.py: require cache_file, mode, use_bond_type in config
  - trainer.py: use direct config access for load_checkpoint
- Rename featurization modules for clarity (atom_features→graph_builder,
  pdb_loading→pdb_loader, pipeline+smiles_loading→mol_featurizer)
- Add coding rules to CLAUDE.md

commit.message:
Optimize ngram materialization to use external sort instead of in-memory cache

Process chunks one-at-a-time and use Unix sort for ordering, avoiding
the need to hold all 3.5GB of ngram data in memory simultaneously.

commit.message:
fix: limit flash-attn build parallelism on ARM64 to avoid OOM

ARM64 c7g.4xlarge (32GB RAM) ran OOM during flash-attn source build
with default MAX_JOBS. Limit to MAX_JOBS=2 so each compiler job
uses ~16GB, staying within the 32GB + 16GB swap budget.

commit.message:
feat(examples): rewrite bf16 GEMV with IRON-style kernel and add profiling (#1360)

Rewrite the bf16 matrix-vector multiplication example with:

- New IRON-style vectorized kernel (r=64, accfloat accumulation,
  reduce_add per row) replacing the 32-bit-word-transposed design
- Row-major A layout (no host-side transpose needed)
- Launch-based outer M tiling with inner m_input loop inside herd
- L2 (MemTile) intermediate buffering for A, B, and C
- Configurable tile sizes: TILE_M_L2, M_INPUT
- XRT profiling harness (test.cpp) with warmup + measurement
- Makefile targets: run, profile, run1/run2, profile1/profile2
- NPU2 LIT test with 2 correctness checks and 2 profiling checks

Verified correct for M=2048,K=8192 and M=8192,K=2048.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace prompt-injection vacation guard with clean TS-layer intercept

Instead of injecting a vacation note into the AI system prompt (which could
affect unrelated responses), add a pure TS intercept right after the user
message is pushed to history:

- If session.data.professionalId is already set and that prof is on vacation:
  - If message contains vacation keywords → return vacation message immediately
    (no AI brain call, no prompt modification)
  - Otherwise → clear professionalId/date so AI naturally suggests alternatives
    (profOptionsVisible already excludes vacationers, so AI knows to offer others)
- Restores customPrompt || undefined in all callBrain invocations
